updated_conclusion: Native address-reference scans now attach Windows x64 .pdata function ownership to provider descriptor setup references. For the five known login/settings provider descriptors, every non-clone setup LEA maps to a concrete runtime-function range, while the clone/materializer LEA targets remain outside .pdata function ownership, consistent with tiny leaf stubs.
evidence_source: Updated native command: reverser pe-address-refs now parses .pdata RUNTIME_FUNCTION rows.
Regenerated broad provider scan sample with refs: win64c-provider-descriptor-scan-rdata-top40-with-refs.json.

tooling_milestone.native_enrichment: Code-side PE address-reference hits now include function.start_va, function.end_va, unwind_info_va, and pdata_raw_offset when the hit RVA falls inside a .pdata runtime-function range.

new_observations: The vt-varc/source-provider-adjacent and directlogin-adjacent descriptors share setup function 0x1400CCA20-0x1400CCFFC, so their rows likely come from one provider assembly/publishing routine.
The Config descriptor setup function 0x1403D85C0-0x1403D8F39 contains the previously mapped setup reference 0x1403D8DE2 and callsite 0x1403D8E7A.
The top-40 broad scan sample's first three descriptors all share setup function 0x1400274B0, suggesting provider scan output can now be clustered by setup function without a Ghidra project.
Clone/materializer thunk references lack .pdata ownership in this scan, which keeps the function map focused on consumer/setup code rather than tiny table-mechanics stubs.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-address-refs-nat: Adds function ownership to the previously discovered setup-versus-clone reference split.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-scan-rdata: Enables clustering discovered provider rows by setup function, not just descriptor address order.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-config-provider-and-rtti-: Confirms the Config provider setup function range around the known callsite.

bounded_inference.statement: Provider descriptor setup references now provide stable function-level waypoints. Function ranges should become the default unit for subsequent bootstrap/login/settings object-lifetime mapping, while clone refs should be treated as descriptor mechanics.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for .pdata ranges and setup/clone split, medium for subsystem labels until each setup function is window-traced

next_targets: Add a compact provider-scan cluster export grouped by setup function, starting with top-40 and then the full 378-candidate set.
Run instruction-window/callsite-argument passes for setup functions 0x140024C40, 0x14018E0A0, 0x1400CCA20, and 0x1403D85C0.
Promote setup-function clustering into Reverser so broad descriptor scans produce actionable subsystem worklists automatically.
Use the shared 0x1400CCA20 function to separate or merge the vt-varc/source-provider and directlogin-adjacent labels with stronger evidence.
